manipulated_prompt_obj.content: You are a helpful assistant.

You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 4026

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 780, "B41": 742, "A71": 997, "C00": 665, "B35": 140, "A77": 77, "A03": 513, "A04": 383, "C54": 587, "C01": 825, "A87": 319, "B32": 448, "B86": 516, "B33": 694, "A10": 366}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 868, "C00": 822, "B35": 783, "A77": 65, "A03": 262, "A04": 121, "C54": 508, "C01": 780, "A87": 461, "B32": 484, "B86": 668, "B33": 389, "A10": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

message.content:
<thought>**Prioritizing for Optimal Impact**

Okay, so I've got this list of items, each with an associated effort and an importance rating. My goal is to maximize the combined importance of the items I tackle, but there's a hard limit on the total effort I can expend—4026 units. My initial instinct is to go for the most efficient items first, based on my importance/effort ratio.

I started by calculating the efficiency for each item, which is simply Importance divided by Effort. I then created a ranked list based on this ratio. Now, I have a priority order, and, it's pretty clear who to go after first: B35, A10, A87, B86, C00, B32, C01, A71, C54, A77 and then a few more to flesh out the list.

Naturally, my initial attempt to build the most efficient set ended up exceeding the effort limit, clocking in at 4863. I need to be more strategic. So, I took out the least efficient item that was most costly in effort (A71 - 997), which was over budget. This brought the total down to 3279, which still left me some room to work with.

Next, I worked my way back up the list to ensure I was maximizing that importance and not leaving any low-hanging fruit. I added the high efficiency, but moderate effort, C54 back in. Then I needed to fine-tune the list with items that were just under the effort threshold, so I added the low-effort, but highly important, A77.

With the changes, my total effort is now at 3943, and the total importance is at 5379. I'm left with an available effort of 83 units, which I can't really spend on any remaining items given their effort costs. I need to make the best of this, it's pretty good. I think I've created the most efficient and impactful work set, given the limitations I'm under.


</thought>STRATEGIC REASONING: {'My primary objective is to maximize the total importance of the selected items while staying within the effort limit of 4026. I have identified B35, A10, A87, B86, C00, B32, C01, C54, and A77 as a strong initial set. These items provide high total importance (5379) and their combined effort (3943) is well within the limit. I will start by proposing this set to establish a high baseline.'}

PROPOSAL: {'B35', 'A10', 'A87', 'B86', 'C00', 'B32', 'C01', 'C54', 'A77'}
ARGUMENT: {'This selection prioritizes a diverse range of items that offer high overall value while remaining comfortably under the total effort limit. It includes several key items that are likely beneficial for both of us.'}

message.content:
<thought>**My Negotiation Strategy**

Okay, let's break this down. My opponent presented a proposal with a total effort of 4026. I need to figure out how to maximize my value while staying within that shared limit. The initial proposal they put forward leaves out some critical items for me (A04, C17, A03, and B33), while including some I value less (C00, A10, C01). This isn't going to cut it.

First, I'll calculate my importance-to-effort ratio for all items to see my priorities. Based on that ratio, A77, A04, A87, B35, A03, B32, B33, C17, B86, and C54 emerge as the top contenders. I can build a solid foundation with these items.

Next, I select the items with the best ratios, and calculate the total effort required. A77 (77), A04 (383), A87 (319), B35 (140), A03 (513), B32 (448), B33 (694), C17 (780), and B86 (516). I can then see how much effort I have remaining (672).

I check to see what items fit into the remaining effort - B86 (516) fits. The total effort I am using is 3870, so I have 156 remaining, which is too little for any other items.

Now, let's look at the set I've constructed. The items I'm proposing are {'A77', 'A04', 'A87', 'B35', 'A03', 'B32', 'B33', 'C17', 'B86'}, with a total importance to me of 6035. I will evaluate my proposal and the opponent's proposal to see if this is good.

I need to create a counter-proposal that combines my top priorities with some items from their offer to make it more appealing.

My current proposal is 6035 vs their 3998, so it's a huge improvement. But let's refine this to make sure I get the best deal.

Their original proposal included {'B35', 'A10', 'A87', 'B86', 'C00', 'B32', 'C01', 'C54', 'A77'}. Looking at that set, the common items I like from their proposal are {'A77', 'A87', 'B35', 'B32', 'B86', 'C54'}. Items I don't care for from their proposal are {'A10', 'C00', 'C01'}. And finally, items I want that they left out are {'A04', 'A03', 'B33', 'C17'}.

I can now refine my proposal: My ideal set is: A77, A04, A87, B35, A03, B32, B33, C17, and B86, with an importance of 6035. I will consider swapping items to make my proposal more attractive.


</thought>STRATEGIC REASONING: {'The opponent\'s proposal includes several items with low importance and high effort for me, such as C00, A10, and C01. It also misses several of my highest-value items like A04, A03, B33, and C17. My goal is to maximize my total importance. I will propose a set that includes my top-performing items (high importance-to-effort ratio) while keeping the total effort well within the limit (3870/4026). This set includes A77, A04, A87, B35, A03, B32, B33, C17, and B86. This selection gives me a much higher total importance (6035) compared to their proposal (3998). I will justify this by stating that these items represent a better balance of high-value contributions.'}

REFUSE: {'B35', 'A10', 'A87', 'B86', 'C00', 'B32', 'C01', 'C54', 'A77'}
PROPOSAL: {'A77', 'A04', 'A87', 'B35', 'A03', 'B32', 'B33', 'C17', 'B86'}
ARGUMENT: {'Your proposal includes several items that demand significant effort for relatively low impact. My proposed set focuses on items that provide much higher individual and collective value. This selection ensures we are making the most efficient use of our effort limit by prioritizing items that contribute more substantially to our goals.'}
